company.name: Corvus Pharmaceuticals
company.subtitle: ITK inhibitor (soquelitinib) · Phase 3 PTCL interim YE2026 · Phase 2 AD starting H1 2026 · ~$235M cash post-offering
company.cashRunway: Into 2028 (~$235M post Jan 2026 $201M raise; ~$60M annual burn)

market.notes: PTCL is small (~$0.5-1B TAM), highly underserved — no fully FDA-approved agents in R/R setting. Phase 3 randomized vs physician's choice (belinostat or pralatrexate), 150 patients, primary = PFS. Final Phase 1/1b ASH Dec 2025 supports activity in PTCL-NOS, CTCL, AITL, ALCL. Interim analysis YE2026 is THE binary catalyst. First-in-class ITK inhibition mechanism.

us.patientsK.note: SEER: ~6K new PTCL + ~3K CTCL/yr US; ~12K prevalent on-tx. Source: NCI SEER 2024, Cutaneous Lymphoma Foundation.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~9K T-cell lymphoma/yr; T-cell Project registry (Italy + EU).

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~40K on-tx; higher PTCL-NOS + ATLL in APAC (HTLV-1).

us.wtpPct.note: ~58% -- Adcetris CD30+ (ALCL) NCCN Cat 1; Poteligeo CCR4+ CTCL; Istodax/Folotyn 2L; Medicare broad.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~42% -- NICE + G-BA approved Adcetris frontline PTCL; ESMO guidelines.

row.wtpPct.note: ~14% -- Japan PMDA (mogamulizumab Japan-origin); China NRDL cHHP + CHOEP.

us.priceK.note: Blended $155K: Adcetris+CHP $175K (40% pts) + Poteligeo $165K (15%) + HDAC/folotyn $125K (15%) + CHOP alone $35K (30%).

row.priceK.note: ~$32K blended; CHOP generic + mogamulizumab Japan NHI.

penPct.note: ~42% peak -- CD30+ Adcetris saturated ALCL; CCR4 Poteligeo CTCL; CD7 CAR-T + JAK/STAT emerging.

us.reachPct.note: R/R PTCL ~5K US; Ph3 post-2L soq vs investigator choice; FDA orphan + fast-track; launch 2028 ~25% reach vs Folotyn/belinostat/Poteligeo.

us.priceK.note: ~$185K/yr oral ITK; anchor to Poteligeo $20K/mo + Folotyn $30K/dose; orphan premium.

us.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- PTCL orphan, NCCN Cat 2A precedent; Medicare Part D + 340B academic centers; LLS copay assist.

eu.reachPct.note: ~18% EU5 PTCL; EMA orphan path; specialist hematology centers only.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~28% -- NICE + G-BA PTCL orphan precedent via Adcetris/Poteligeo.

row.reachPct.note: ~10% ROW; Japan PMDA Poteligeo (Kyowa) entrenched T-cell lymphoma.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- Japan NHI hematology orphan; China NRDL Adcetris 2021 precedent.

market.notes: AD market $15-20B (Dupixent ~$13B, Rinvoq, Cibinqo, Adbry, Ebglyss). Phase 1 cohort 4 (Jan 2026): 200mg BID over 56 days, deepening responses, activity in dupilumab- and JAKi-failed patients, favorable safety. Stock 10x'd on data. ITK MOA may differentiate vs JAKi safety. Oral but BID dosing may limit adoption vs Dupixent SC q2w. Phase 2 designed as pivotal-enabling.

us.patientsK.note: NEA: ~1.5M US moderate-severe AD on systemic therapy; ~16.5M US adults have AD symptoms total.

eu.patientsK.note: ETFAD: ~2.5M EU27+UK moderate-severe AD systemic-eligible; EASI/IGA severity scoring.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~20M mod-severe; Japan high prevalence + strong Dupixent uptake; China rapid growth.

us.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- Dupixent commercial/Medicare Part D; Rinvoq/Cibinqo JAKi; Adbry (tralokinumab); Ebglyss (lebrikizumab) new.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~48% -- NICE/G-BA cover Dupixent + JAKi restricted use (BBW); Adbry/Ebglyss rolling out.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- Japan J-DPS + China NRDL added Dupixent; cyclosporine generic + steroids baseline elsewhere.

us.priceK.note: Blended $38K: Dupixent $40K + Rinvoq $65K + Cibinqo $60K + Adbry $40K + Ebglyss $40K; cyclosporine generic $3K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$22K blended -- Dupixent net ~$25K + JAKi ~$30K; cyclosporine/MTX generic backbone.

row.priceK.note: ~$8K -- cyclosporine generic + phototherapy; biologics <15% penetration ex-Japan.

penPct.note: ~35% peak -- rapid biologic/JAKi expansion; OX40 (rocatinlimab, amlitelimab) pipeline adding options.

us.reachPct.note: Moderate-severe AD ~7M US adults; post-Dupixent pool ~2M; ITK oral novel MoA vs JAK (Rinvoq) + IL-13 (Ebglyss); reach ~8% by yr5 given crowded market.

us.priceK.note: ~$42K/yr oral; anchor to Rinvoq $70K + Cibinqo $40K; undercut biologic Dupixent $38K WAC.

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- Dupixent benchmark commercial + Medicare Part D; JAK boxed warning helps oral ITK positioning; PA required post-Dupixent fail.

eu.reachPct.note: ~15% EU5 moderate-severe AD post-NICE TA + G-BA; Dupixent + Rinvoq entrenched; ITK niche post-JAK.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~25% -- NICE restrictive post-Dupixent; G-BA Cibinqo/Rinvoq precedent.

row.reachPct.note: ~6% ROW; Japan PMDA Rinvoq/Cibinqo well-entrenched; China NRDL Dupixent 2023.

row.wtpPct.note: ~12% -- Japan NHI oral AD broad; China AD biologic access expanding.

market.notes: Severe biologic asthma ~$10-15B (Dupixent asthma ~$5B, Xolair ~$2B, Nucala ~$2B, Tezspire ramping). ITK mechanism blocks Th2 cytokines upstream — rationale for biologic-resistant / Th2-high severe asthma. Oral convenience vs SC biologics is major differentiation if efficacy holds. 4th-line setting realistic initial position.

penPct.note: Peak penetration inherited from legacy config -- refine per competitive landscape.

us.reachPct.note: Severe asthma ~2.5M US uncontrolled; Tezspire/Nucala/Fasenra/Dupixent entrenched; ITK oral differentiator vs injectable biologics, reach ~6% peak.

us.priceK.note: ~$38K/yr oral asthma; anchored below biologic SOC for oral convenience premium.

us.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- Dupixent asthma $38K + Tezspire $42K benchmark; oral convenience value but biologic efficacy bar high.

eu.reachPct.note: ~12% EU5; NICE TA allergic/eosinophilic severe asthma biologic-dominant; ITK oral gated.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~22% -- GINA + NICE biologic algorithms restrict oral adds.

row.reachPct.note: ~5% ROW; Japan Dupixent/Nucala well-covered; China NRDL asthma biologic entrenched.

market.notes: HS market ~$1-2B growing — Humira, Cosentyx, Adbry approved. Severely underserved — most patients on multi-line failure. Th17-driven disease, ITK blocks Th17 development upstream. Oral for a disease treated primarily with injectables is strong differentiator. Small but high unmet need.

us.patientsK.note: HS Foundation: ~200K US moderate-severe HS; ~1% US prevalence estimated; underdiagnosed by 7-10yr delay.

eu.patientsK.note: EADV HS registry: ~300K EU27+UK mod-severe HS; Hurley staging + IHS4 severity.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~2M mod-severe HS; massively underdiagnosed globally; emerging awareness programs.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- adalimumab biosim first-line (EMA HS approval) + Cosentyx + Bimzelx; clindamycin/rifampin combos baseline.

row.wtpPct.note: ~10% -- antibiotics + surgery; biologics minimal ex-Japan; Humira biosim entry expanding access.

us.priceK.note: Blended $45K: Humira biosim $18K + Cosentyx $60K + Bimzelx $75K + clindamycin/rifampin $2K baseline.

eu.priceK.note: ~$22K blended -- adalimumab biosim ~$8K net + Cosentyx ~$30K + Bimzelx ~$35K.

penPct.note: ~25% peak -- IL-17 (Cosentyx/Bimzelx) expanded ladder; povorcitinib/sonelokimab pipeline accelerating.

us.reachPct.note: Hidradenitis suppurativa ~150K US mod-severe; Humira + Cosentyx approved, Bimzelx Ph3 positive; ITK oral Ph2 exploratory, reach ~7% peak.

us.priceK.note: ~$40K/yr oral; anchored below HS biologics for convenience; HS orphan designation possible.

us.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- Humira $90K HS indication + Cosentyx $70K; HS Foundation advocacy; commercial + Medicaid broad.

eu.reachPct.note: ~12% EU5 HS; NICE TA adalimumab SOC; specialist dermatology HS centers.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~20% -- NICE TA392 Humira HS precedent; G-BA HS orphan access.

row.reachPct.note: <5% ROW; Japan HS awareness low; China biologic access expanding.

mega_bear.narrative: PTCL Phase 3 fails AND AD Phase 2 disappoints — soquelitinib platform invalidated
events: ✗ PTCL Phase 3 interim shows insufficient PFS benefit vs belinostat/pralatrexate
✗ AD Phase 2 fails to replicate Phase 1 cohort 4 efficacy
✗ Asthma + HS programs shelved
→ Stock collapses to cash floor (~$2.80/share — 52w low territory)

bear.narrative: PTCL hits but AD Phase 2 modest — small oncology launch + uncertain inflam franchise
events: ~ PTCL Phase 3 modestly positive — small label, limited commercial
✗ AD Phase 2 efficacy below Dupixent benchmark
✗ Oral BID dosing fails to displace SC biologics
→ R/R PTCL launch ~$200-400M peak, AD shelved, cash + small onc

base.narrative: PTCL Phase 3 positive + AD Phase 2 confirms — first-in-class ITK platform validated
events: ✓ PTCL Phase 3 interim hits PFS — first targeted therapy with OS-supportive data in R/R PTCL
✓ AD Phase 2 replicates cohort 4 — competitive efficacy + clean safety vs JAKi
~ Asthma + HS Phase 2 reads out 2027 with positive signals
→ Onc launch 2028, AD pivotal start 2027, BLA ~2030

bull.narrative: ITK mechanism validated across onc + multiple inflammatory diseases — first-in-class platform
events: ✓ PTCL Phase 3 strong — best-in-class for R/R PTCL, label expansion to CTCL
✓ AD Phase 2 best-in-class oral — Phase 3 starts 2027
✓ Asthma + HS PoC positive — multi-indication platform
✓ M&A target: Pfizer / Sanofi for derm/inflammation franchise

psychedelic_bull.narrative: ITK = new immunology pillar — soquelitinib becomes platform across onc + multiple Th2/Th17 diseases — Big Pharma acquires
events: ✓ PTCL Phase 3 best-in-class — establishes ITK as oncology mechanism
✓ AD Phase 3 differentiates as best oral — Dupixent share gain
✓ Asthma + HS both positive — first multi-indication ITK franchise
✓ Pfizer or Sanofi acquires for $40-60/share to own ITK class

lastData.title: AD Phase 1 cohort 4 + final PTCL Phase 1/1b ASH data
points: AD cohort 4 (Jan 2026): 200mg BID × 56 days, deepening response, activity in Dupixent and JAKi failures
Cohort 4 designed prospectively to extend cohorts 1-3 — favorable safety/efficacy consistent
$201M upsized public offering closed Jan 23, 2026 at ~$22 — fully funds through interim
Final Phase 1/1b PTCL data ASH Dec 2025: activity across PTCL-NOS, CTCL, AITL, ALCL
Phase 3 PTCL registrational: 150 pts, vs belinostat/pralatrexate, primary PFS, ongoing
Phase 2 AD planned ~200 pts, 12-week treatment, multiple dose arms — starts Q1/Q2 2026
Q4 2025 cash $65.7M + $190M net raise = ~$235M cash, runway into 2028
March 2026: $200M shelf filed (Jefferies ATM) for additional financing capacity

nextCatalyst.title: PTCL Phase 3 interim analysis
thresholds: Primary endpoint: progression-free survival vs physician's choice
First registrational data for ITK inhibition in oncology
Also 2026: AAD/SID biomarker presentations, Phase 2 AD initiation, asthma/HS PoC starts

risks: PTCL Phase 3 is binary — Phase 1/1b N=23 is thin Phase 3 predictor
Single asset (soquelitinib) — diversification is across indications, not molecules
AD Phase 1 was N=~20 per cohort — Phase 2 (200pt) translation risk
Oral BID dosing harder to compete vs Dupixent SC q2w if efficacy parity
$200M ATM filed — additional dilution likely as multiple Ph2 trials run in parallel
ITK inhibition is unproven mechanism — no precedent for class effects
PTCL is small (~$0.5-1B) — Phase 3 success doesn't justify $1.3B mcap alone, AD must work
Stock 10x'd YTD — significant momentum unwind risk on any stumble
Insider concentration: Thompson 10.5%, Miller 3.1%, insiders 39% total — low float dynamics
Platform optionality (lupus, MS, T1D exploratory) not modeled — upside beyond current assets

catalysts.title: Phase 2 atopic dermatitis trial initiation (~200 pts)
Severe asthma Phase 2 PoC trial initiation
Hidradenitis suppurativa Phase 2 PoC trial initiation
